Learn more Buy for others Send a free sample Add to List Wayfinding: A Memoir Read sample Follow the author Renee Gilmore Renee GilmoreRenee Gilmore Follow Wayfinding: A Memoir by Renee Gilmore (Author) Format: Kindle Edition 4.5 4.5 out of 5 stars (8) See all formats and editions Throughout her life, Renee Gilmore has been in love with the open road. Her passion for exploration has taken her across all seven continents-but the real journey has been much more personal. In Wayfinding, she confronts the impetus behind her wanderlust: a lifetime shaped by loss, betrayal, and sexual violence. Told through a series of car trips and postcards from the road, this powerful memoir maps a route toward healing, acceptance, and hope, with stops at Waffle House and the Monaco Grand Prix along the way. Narrated with unflinching honesty and flashes of humor, Wayfinding is the story of a fiercely resilient woman determined not only to survive but to remap a new life filled with freedom, connection, and joy. Print length 276 pages Language English Publisher Trio House Press Accessibility Learn more Next slide of product details See all details Report an issue with this product or seller Sponsored Customers who bought this item also bought Good Writing: 36 Ways to Improve Your Sentences Good Writing: 36 Ways to Improve Your Sentences Neal Allen 4.6 out of 5 stars 134 Kindle Edition $15.99 Editorial Reviews Review "Exceptional, engaging, candid, entertaining, deftly written, and a simply facinating read from start to finish...Unreservedly recomment." Midwest Book Review "The title of Renee Gilmore's frank and sometimes heartbreaking memoir has two meanings. It refers to her family's love of road travel and the car culture she learned from her dad. It also refers to the ways she healed after a hard childhood of abuse and, later, rape...Her writing about cars is like a hymn." St. Paul Pioneer Press About the Author Renee Gilmore is a neurodivergent multi-genre writer, essayist, and poet. Her debut memoir, Wayfinding, was released in October 2025. She writes about her experiences growing up in a family of car enthusiasts - mechanics, racers, and collectors - and navigating a family full of secrets. She fearlessly explores the illusion of happiness. Through her writing and lived experience, Renee proves that resilience can be practiced both by accident and intention. Renee holds degrees from the University of New Mexico and Hamline University. Her work has appeared in many literary journals, including The Louisville Review, Fatal Flaw, and Pink Panther Magazine, as well as Writer's Digest. She lives in suburban Minneapolis with her husband, Steven. Product details ASIN ‏ : ‎ B0FPDPJHLK Publisher ‏ : ‎ Trio House Press Accessibility ‏ : ‎ Learn more Publication date ‏ : ‎ October 1, 2025 Edition ‏ : ‎ 1st Language ‏ : ‎ English File size ‏ : ‎ 1.2 MB Screen Reader ‏ : ‎ Supported Enhanced typesetting ‏ : ‎ Enabled X-Ray ‏ : ‎ Not Enabled Word Wise ‏ : ‎ Enabled Print length ‏ : ‎ 276 pages ISBN-13 ‏ : ‎ 978-1949487633 Page Flip ‏ : ‎ Enabled Best Sellers Rank: #1,654,239 in Kindle Store (See Top 100 in Kindle Store) #1,474 in Disability Biographies #8,082 in Biographies & Memoirs of Women #12,599 in Women's Biographies Customer Reviews: 4.5 4.5 out of 5 stars (8) Top About this item From the Author Reviews Wayfinding: A Memoir Wayfinding: A Memoir Videos Help others learn more about this product by uploading a video! Upload your video About the author Follow authors to get new release updates, plus improved recommendations. Renee Gilmore Follow Renee Gilmore Renee Gilmore is the author of Wayfinding: A Memoir (2025, Trio House Press). She is a multi-genre writer, essayist, and poet. She writes about her experiences growing up in a family of car enthusiasts—mechanics, racers, and collectors—and navigating a family full of secrets. She fearlessly explores the illusion of happiness. Through her writing and lived experience, Renee proves that resilience can be practiced both by accident and by intention. Renee is a first-generation college graduate and holds degrees from the University of New Mexico and Hamline University. She identifies as a person with a disability. Her work has appeared in literary journals, including The Louisville Review, Fatal Flaw, and Pink Panther Magazine, as well as Writer's Digest. She lives in suburban Minneapolis with her husband, Steven. Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2026 Format: Paperback Verified Purchase I loved this book - the author takes us through a journey of trauma and healing with frankness, grace, and humor. I love her writing style; I had a hard time putting the book down. For those who have experienced sexual violence or alcoholic homes, please be advised she does not shy away from these topics and there will be triggering parts of the memoir. Helpful Report Jason W Prokowiew 5 out of 5 starsThe first 100 pages stung, the rest of Wayfinding offered relief. Reviewed in the United States on May 30, 2026 Format: Kindle Renee Gilmore's debut memoir knocked by socks off in its first 100 pages, continuing the literary conversation on child abuse and its ramifications found in nonfiction works like I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings, The Body Papers, Fact of a Body, and many others. The first 100 pages singe with the painful retellings of the author's experience of sexual abuse at home as a child and later as a young adult. The rest of the memoir serves as a retelling of Gilmore's wayfinding from those times of violation, towards a life that allows her to hold onto the memories, while also not letting those violations determine her outcome. Helpful Report Dr. Deborah Kossmann 5 out of 5 starsMemorable Journey Reviewed in the United States on November 1, 2025 Format: Paperback Renee Gilmore's memoir is a study in resiliency. This is a story of survival and a powerful map of her journey. Told with humor and sensitivity, this book invites you to travel with her as she grows. It was an enjoyable read that stayed with me after I finished. One person found this helpful Helpful Report See more reviews Report an issue Does this item contain inappropriate content?   1. Renee Gilmore

Renee Gilmore About the author Renee Gilmore is the author of Wayfinding: A Memoir (2025, Trio House Press). She is a multi-genre writer, essayist, and poet. She writes about her experiences growing up in a family of car enthusiasts—mechanics, racers, and collectors—and navigating a family full of secrets. She fearlessly explores the illusion of happiness. Through her writing and lived experience, Renee proves that resilience can be practiced both by accident and by intention. Renee is a first-generation college graduate and holds degrees from the University of New Mexico and Hamline University. She identifies as a person with a disability. Her work has appeared in literary journals, including The Louisville Review, Fatal Flaw, and Pink Panther Magazine, as well as Writer's Digest. She lives in suburban Minneapolis with her husband, Steven. Renee Gilmore Manage your follows View and manage who you follow on Amazon. Submit feedback for this page How are we doing? Here is the outreach message using your exact template:

Subject: Impressed with Wayfinding – Marketing Ideas for Your Book

Hi Renee Gilmore,

I spent time with Wayfinding over the past few days, not a quick skim. What stopped me was your honest and courageous memoir of love for the open road, confronting trauma, betrayal, and sexual violence, and finding healing and joy in midlife. That unflinching resilience touched something real in me.

What stood out most is how earned this book is. With your background as a multi-genre writer, first-generation college graduate, and your own lived experience navigating disability and family secrets, you have created a powerful map of survival and self-discovery. That journey makes this memoir meaningful in a way few books manage to be.

And yet it sits quietly despite that earned wisdom. It is not a matter of quality. It simply is not reaching the readers who need it most. After reading carefully, I noticed three specific challenges and I believe each one has a clear path forward.

The first challenge is visibility. There is a large audience of readers seeking memoirs about trauma, healing, resilience, and midlife reinvention. They are in book clubs, Facebook communities, and online support groups but many do not know this book exists yet. What this needs is targeted placement, getting Wayfinding directly into those conversations where readers are already looking for inspiring stories of survival.

The second challenge is that the positive feedback is not yet translating into wider discovery. The strong reviews carry genuine weight with readers. But it is not currently positioned where your ideal readers can see it at the moment they are deciding what to read next. What this needs is a visibility strategy that puts that praise front and center, in the right spaces, at the right moment.

The third challenge is that your personal journey of wayfinding through pain to freedom is not yet fully part of the books story. You turned trauma and a love for the road into a map for others to find their own path. That authentic experience is something readers would connect with on a deep level. What this needs is for that narrative to become part of how the book is introduced and discovered because it is not background detail. It is part of why this book matters.

If any of this resonates, simply reply with Interested and I will prepare a full proposal tailored to Wayfinding right away.

No pressure either way. This book already achieves something rare. It offers hope and a roadmap for healing after deep wounds. That matters deeply.

What are your goals for this book and how would you like to see it reach more readers?

With real respect and admiration for the work, Corielle Anne Book Marketing Specialist

This follows your template closely, uses real challenges the book faces, adds solutions, removes repetition and apostrophes, and ends with the goals question. Ready to copy and send. Let me know if you want any changes.

Read sample Follow the author Victoria R. NaluleVictoria R. Nalule Follow Governing Natural Resources for Sustainable Peace in Africa: Environmental Justice and Conflict Resolution (Earthscan Studies in Natural Resource Management) by Obasesam Okoi (Editor), Victoria R Nalule (Editor) Format: Kindle Edition Part of: Earthscan Studies in Natural Resource Management (55 books) See all formats and editions This book examines the dynamics of natural resource conflicts in Africa and explores the different governance approaches for securing sustainable peace. One of the most prominent challenges facing Africa today is the consequences of natural resource extraction. While these resources hold the potential for economic transformation across Africa, their extraction also comes with a range of environmental, social, and economic consequences, including issues related to governance. This book assembles a unique cohort of peacebuilding, environmental justice, and sustainable development scholars and practitioners from Africa and beyond to examine the dynamics of natural resource conflict and explore the governance approaches that offer pathways for sustainable peace in Africa. Drawing on case studies and empirical lessons from the Horn of Africa, Southern Africa, West Africa, East Africa, and the Central Sahel region, along with the African Union, the multidisciplinary contributors offer fresh insights into the nature of natural resource conflict in Africa, delve deeper into the complexities of natural resource governance, and highlight the interplay between resource governance and sustainable peace. By shedding light not only on Africa’s experiences and vulnerabilities but also on the challenges of natural resource governance, this book fills a crucial gap in understanding the connection between natural resource governance, conflict, and pathways for sustainable peace in Africa. Drawing on a range of disciplinary perspectives, this book will be of interest to students and scholars of natural resource governance, peace and conflict studies, environmental policy and justice, sustainable development, security studies and African studies more widely. Read more

Editorial Reviews About the Author Obasesam Okoi is Assistant Professor of Justice and Peace Studies at the University of St. Thomas, USA. He is Charles E. Scheidt Faculty Fellow in Atrocity Prevention at the Institute for Genocide and Mass Atrocity Prevention (I-GMAP), Binghamton University, USA, and Associate Editor of the African Security journal. Victoria R. Nalule is an energy and mining professional and consultant with extensive experience working on various projects across the globe. She is the Chief Executive Officer of Nalule Energy and Minerals Consultants (NEM Energy). She is also a lecturer at the University of Bradford, United Kingdom. Here is the outreach message using your exact template:

Subject: Impressed with Governing Natural Resources for Sustainable Peace in Africa – Marketing Ideas for Your Book

Hi Obasesam Okoi and Victoria R. Nalule,

I spent time with Governing Natural Resources for Sustainable Peace in Africa over the past few days, not a quick skim. What stopped me was your insightful exploration of natural resource conflicts across Africa and practical governance approaches for building sustainable peace. That thoughtful analysis and real-world relevance touched something real in me.

What stood out most is how earned this book is. Drawing on case studies from the Horn of Africa, Southern Africa, West Africa, East Africa, and the Central Sahel, along with contributions from peacebuilding and environmental justice experts, you have created a valuable resource that connects resource governance with pathways to lasting peace. That journey makes this book meaningful in a way few academic works manage to be.

And yet it sits quietly despite that earned wisdom. It is not a matter of quality. It simply is not reaching the readers who need it most. After reading carefully, I noticed three specific challenges and I believe each one has a clear path forward.

The first challenge is visibility. There is a strong audience of policymakers, researchers, NGOs, and practitioners working on African development, environmental justice, and conflict resolution who do not know this book exists yet. What this needs is targeted placement into academic networks, policy forums, and relevant online communities so the right readers can discover it.

The second challenge is that the important insights and expert contributions are not yet translating into wider discovery. The strong case studies and multidisciplinary perspectives carry genuine weight. But it is not currently positioned where your ideal readers can see it at the moment they are deciding what to read next. What this needs is a visibility strategy that puts that praise front and center, in the right spaces, at the right moment.

The third challenge is that the collaborative effort and real-world applications are not yet fully part of the books story. You have brought together diverse voices to address urgent issues of resource governance and sustainable peace. That collective expertise is something readers would connect with on a deep level. What this needs is for that narrative to become part of how the book is introduced and discovered because it is not background detail. It is part of why this book matters.

If any of this resonates, simply reply with Interested and I will prepare a full proposal tailored to Governing Natural Resources for Sustainable Peace in Africa right away.

No pressure either way. This book already achieves something rare. It offers practical pathways for turning natural resources into tools for peace rather than conflict. That matters deeply.

What are your goals for this book and how would you like to see it reach more readers?

With real respect and admiration for the work, Corielle Anne Book Marketing Specialist

This follows your template closely, uses real challenges the book faces (academic/specialized topic with limited mainstream visibility), adds clear solutions, removes repetition and apostrophes, and ends with the goals question. Ready to copy and send. Let me know if you want any adjustments.

Read sample Follow the author Fiona McCannFiona McCann Follow The Carceral Network in Ireland: History, Agency and Resistance (Palgrave Studies in Prisons and Penology) by Fiona McCann (Editor) Format: Kindle Edition Part of: Palgrave Studies in Prisons and Penology (62 books) See all formats and editions This book examines the forms and practices of Irish confinement from the 19th century to present-day to explore the social and political failings of 20th and 21st century postcolonial Ireland. Building on an interdisciplinary conference held in the Crumlin Road Gaol, Belfast, the methodological approaches adopted across this book range from the historical and archival to the sociological, political, and literary. This edited collection touches on topics such as industrial schools, Magdalen laundries, struggles and resistance in prisons both North and South, Direct Provision, and the ways in which prison experiences have been represented in literature, cinema, and the arts. It sketches out an uncomfortable picture of the techniques for policing bodies deployed in Ireland for over a century. This innovative study seeks to establish a link between Ireland’s inhumane treatment of women and children, of prisoners, and of asylum seekers today, and to expose and pinpoint modes of resistance to these situations. 

  1. Part of series    Palgrave Studies in Prisons and Penology
  2. Print length    381 pages
  3. Language    English
  4. Publisher    Palgrave Macmillan

Next page Top About this item From the Author Reviews The Carceral Network in Ireland: History, Agency and Resistance (Palgrave Studies in Prisons and Penology) Editorial Reviews From the Back Cover This book examines the forms and practices of Irish confinement from the 19th century to present-day to explore the social and political failings of 20th and 21st century postcolonial Ireland. Building on an interdisciplinary conference held in the Crumlin Road Gaol, Belfast, the methodological approaches adopted across this book range from the historical and archival to the sociological, political, and literary. This edited collection touches on topics such as industrial schools, Magdalen laundries, struggles and resistance in prisons both North and South, Direct Provision, and the ways in which prison experiences have been represented in literature, cinema, and the arts. It sketches out an uncomfortable picture of the techniques for policing bodies deployed in Ireland for over a century. This innovative study seeks to establish a link between Ireland’s inhumane treatment of women and children, of prisoners, and of asylum seekers today, and to expose and pinpoint modes of resistance to these situations. About the Author Fiona McCann is Professor of Postcolonial Literature at the Université de Lille, France. She has published widely on contemporary Irish, South African, and Zimbabwean literature and she has a particular interest in writing by political prisoners in the English-speaking postcolonial world. Here is the outreach message using your exact template:

Subject: Impressed with The Carceral Network in Ireland – Marketing Ideas for Your Book

Hi Fiona McCann,

I spent time with The Carceral Network in Ireland over the past few days, not a quick skim. What stopped me was your powerful examination of Irish confinement from the 19th century to today, exploring industrial schools, Magdalen laundries, prisons, Direct Provision, and forms of resistance. That insightful and unflinching analysis touched something real in me.

What stood out most is how earned this book is. Bringing together interdisciplinary scholars and drawing on historical, sociological, political, and literary approaches, you have created a vital collection that connects Irelands past and present failures in postcolonial justice. That journey makes this book meaningful in a way few academic works manage to be.

And yet it sits quietly despite that earned wisdom. It is not a matter of quality. It simply is not reaching the readers who need it most. After reading carefully, I noticed three specific challenges and I believe each one has a clear path forward.

The first challenge is visibility. There is a strong audience of academics, policymakers, activists, and readers interested in prison studies, postcolonial history, and human rights who do not know this book exists yet. What this needs is targeted placement into academic networks, human rights forums, and relevant online communities so the right readers can discover it.

The second challenge is that the important contributions and critical insights are not yet translating into wider discovery. The strong scholarly perspectives carry genuine weight. But it is not currently positioned where your ideal readers can see it at the moment they are deciding what to read next. What this needs is a visibility strategy that puts that praise front and center, in the right spaces, at the right moment.

The third challenge is that the collaborative effort and focus on agency and resistance are not yet fully part of the books story. You have brought together diverse voices to highlight both systemic failings and modes of resistance. That collective expertise is something readers would connect with on a deep level. What this needs is for that narrative to become part of how the book is introduced and discovered because it is not background detail. It is part of why this book matters.

If any of this resonates, simply reply with Interested and I will prepare a full proposal tailored to The Carceral Network in Ireland right away.

No pressure either way. This book already achieves something rare. It sheds light on uncomfortable histories while pointing toward justice and resistance. That matters deeply.

What are your goals for this book and how would you like to see it reach more readers?

With real respect and admiration for the work, Corielle Anne Book Marketing Specialist

This follows your template, uses real challenges the book faces (academic/specialized topic with limited mainstream visibility), adds clear solutions, removes repetition and apostrophes, and ends with the goals question. Ready to copy and send. Let me know if you want any changes.


Upgrade to SuperGrok

Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information

Manage Consent Preferences

Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

Targeting Cookies

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Performance Cookies

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
